name: LapEE OS reference UKI baseline
description: The LapEE reference OS (lapee-baremetal) boots a systemd-stub UKI built from an immutable kernel image pinned against a verified source hash. The kernel is built with CONFIG_SECURITY_LOCKDOWN_LSM=y + CONFIG_LOCKDOWN_CONFIDENTIALITY=y, CONFIG_IMA=y with a mandatory appraisal policy, CONFIG_INIT_ON_ALLOC_DEFAULT_ON=y, CONFIG_INIT_ON_FREE_DEFAULT_ON=y, CONFIG_SLAB_MERGE_DEFAULT=n, and halts in head_64.S if TME-MK init returns failure.
pcr-extension-order: pcr-11: UKI PE image (the kernel-integrity boundary)
pcr-12: EV_IPL key=value for each systemd-stub parameter
pcr-15: runtime-extended by lapee-node on successful boot
notes: Tier-3 evidence for claim.tme.enabled, claim.lockdown.confidentiality, and claim.kernel-integrity fires automatically when a quote's PCR 11 value matches any hash in `known-uki-hashes' below, OR when the event log contains an EV_IPL `kernel_name=lapee-*'. The hash list is empty here; real deployment populates it at release time from the UKI ESP entry's SHA-256.
